title: Replace static ExternalSecrets with operator projection
description:
# GitHub Issue #443: feat(c5c3-operator): replace the remaining static ExternalSecrets (keystone-admin, mariadb-root-password, k-orc-clouds-yaml) with operator-owned projection

**Description:**

`deploy/eso/externalsecrets/` still ships three statically applied ExternalSecrets — `keystone-admin.yaml`, `mariadb-root-password.yaml`, and `k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml`, all listed in `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml` and pulled into the production stack via `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml:23` (`- ../../eso`). Each pins the default Quick Start identity (ControlPlane `controlplane` in namespace `openstack`), so bringing up a ControlPlane under any other name or namespace requires editing static manifests. This issue gives them the CC-0116 treatment that PR #442 (commit `407e8580`) established for `keystone-db`: operator-owned per-ControlPlane projection where a per-CR credential exists, production deletion plus a kind-only shim where only the standalone/dev flow needs the default identity, and outright consumer elimination where the manifest backs nothing on the critical path.

The work splits into one operator feature (Secret 1, the bulk of the change), one manifest relocation (Secret 2), and one consumer removal (Secret 3). The concrete boundaries:

**1. The structural template already exists; this issue adds its admin-password sibling.**
`operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_dbcredentials.go` is the merged CC-0116 pattern: a name-suffix constant (`dbCredentialSecretNameSuffix = "-db-credentials"`, line 29), a remote-key helper (`dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor`, lines 35-37), a name helper (`dbCredentialSecretName`, lines 42-44), a pure builder returning an `esov1.ExternalSecret` (the `external-secrets.io/v1` type, **not** `v1alpha1`) with `SecretStoreRef{Kind: "ClusterSecretStore", Name: openBaoClusterStoreName}` and `Target.CreationPolicy: esov1.CreatePolicyOwner` in `childNamespace(cp)` (`dbCredentialExternalSecret`, lines 52-67; `childNamespace(cp)` returns `cp.Namespace`, `reconcile_infrastructure.go:43-45`), and the sub-reconciler itself (`reconcileDBCredentials`, lines 83-150): brownfield early-exit, `controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate` with `SetControllerReference` inside the mutate closure (GC wired), then a `secrets.WaitForExternalSecret` gate (`internal/common/secrets/secrets.go:45-60` — it checks the **ExternalSecret object's** Ready condition, so a plain Secret can never satisfy it) requeuing with `dbCredentialsRequeueAfter` (10s, `requeue_intervals.go:23`).

This issue adds `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_adminpassword.go`, a 1:1 mirror with these names (used consistently below):
- `adminPasswordSecretNameSuffix = "-admin-credentials"` (with the same `//nolint:gosec` G101 false-positive annotation the DB suffix carries).
- `adminPasswordSecretName(cp) = keystoneName(cp) + adminPasswordSecretNameSuffix` — default identity resolves to `controlplane-keystone-admin-credentials` (`keystoneName(cp) = cp.Name + "-keystone"`, `reconcile_keystone.go:29,41-43`).
- `adminPasswordRemoteKeyFor(cp) = fmt.Sprintf("bootstrap/%s/%s/admin", cp.Namespace, keystoneName(cp))` — default identity resolves to `bootstrap/openstack/controlplane-keystone/admin`, exactly the path today's `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-admin.yaml` reads. **Path-scoping nuance:** the admin path is keystone-name-scoped (`{ns}/{keystoneName(cp)}/admin`), unlike the cp-name-scoped DB path (`openstack/keystone/{ns}/{cp.Name}/db`). This matches both the seeder (`write-bootstrap-secrets.sh` seeds `kv-v2/bootstrap/${cp_ns}/${cp_name}-keystone/admin`) and the keystone-operator's Model B rotation PushSecret, whose RemoteKey is `bootstrap/%s/%s/admin` over `keystone.Namespace`/`keystone.Name` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_passwordrotation.go:604`) — and the projected Keystone child is named `keystoneName(cp)` in `cp.Namespace`, so read path and rotation write path land on the same key.
- `adminPasswordExternalSecret(cp)` — pure builder, one data entry `{SecretKey: "password", RemoteRef: {Key: adminPasswordRemoteKeyFor(cp), Property: "password"}}`, `RefreshInterval` 1h, owner reference left to the reconciler's mutate closure, mirroring `dbCredentialExternalSecret` line for line.
- `reconcileAdminPassword(ctx, cp)` — brownfield early-exit on `cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef == nil` (same discriminator as `reconcileDBCredentials`, per the issue) setting the condition `True` with reason `BrownfieldUserSuppliedCredential`; managed path `CreateOrUpdate` + `WaitForExternalSecret`, requeuing with a new `adminPasswordRequeueAfter = 10 * time.Second` in `requeue_intervals.go` (dedicated constant per the per-sub-reconciler naming convention documented at `requeue_intervals.go:18-23`), with condition reasons `ExternalSecretError`, `WaitingForAdminPasswordSecret` (not-ready), and `AdminPasswordReady` (ready) mirroring the DB reasons.

The per-CP-unique name is mandatory, not stylistic: `childNamespace(cp) == cp.Namespace`, so two ControlPlanes in one namespace would collide on a single `keystone-admin` Secret — the "Hardcoded sub-resource names prevent multi-instance support" failure CC-0116 fixed with `-db-credentials`.

**2. Condition and instrumentation wiring is established for six sub-reconcilers; this issue registers the seventh.**
- Add `conditionTypeAdminPasswordReady = "AdminPasswordReady"` to the constants block at `controlplane_controller.go:46-54`. The name must not collide with the existing `conditionTypeAdminCredentialReady = "AdminCredentialReady"` (line 51), which belongs to the K-ORC ApplicationCredential sub-reconciler — these are different credentials.
- Append it to `subConditionTypes` (`controlplane_controller.go:58-65`) so `setReadyCondition` (lines 168-186, via `conditions.AllTrue`) gates the aggregate `Ready` on it automatically.
- Add `"AdminPassword": conditionTypeAdminPasswordReady` to `subReconcilerConditionTypes` (`instrumentation.go:40-47`) so `instrumentSubReconciler` resolves the `condition_type` metric label instead of falling back to the `subReconcilerConditionTypeUnknown` (`"UNKNOWN"`) sentinel (`instrumentation.go:26,81-84`).
- Insert the chain call in `Reconcile` between the `DBCredentials` block (`controlplane_controller.go:111-115`) and the `Keystone` block (lines 117-121), using the same `instrumentSubReconciler(ctx, "AdminPassword", …)` + `!result.IsZero() || err != nil` early-return shape. Running before Keystone is load-bearing: the keystone-operator's `reconcileSecrets` gates `SecretsReady` on the admin Secret being backed by a **Ready ExternalSecret** (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go:119-133`, `WaitForExternalSecret(adminSecretKey)`, reason `WaitingForAdminCredentials`), so the ExternalSecret must exist before the Keystone child references it.
- The existing drift guard `TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames` (`instrumentation_test.go:209`) asserts every map value is a member of `subConditionTypes` and must pass with the new entry. Note for the implementer: c5c3's `instrumentation.go` comments (lines 22, 36, 67) also cite a `TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllCallSites` AST guard, but that test exists only in the keystone operator (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/instrumentation_test.go`) — the issue's "CC-0089 drift-guard / condition-coverage tests" maps to `TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames` plus the `TestInstrumentSubReconciler_*` suite in this package; porting the AST guard is out of scope (Non-Goals).

**3. The defaulting webhook stays untouched; the projected child gets a managed-mode override.**
`controlplane_webhook.go` materializes `cp.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential.PasswordSecretRef = {DefaultAdminPasswordSecretName: "keystone-admin" (line 57), DefaultAdminPasswordSecretKey: "password" (line 64)}` at lines 159-164 — these defaults remain, exactly as `DefaultDatabaseSecretName = "keystone-db"` (line 46) survived CC-0116. What changes is the projection: `reconcile_keystone.go:131` currently passes the cp-level ref through verbatim (`keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef = cp.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential.PasswordSecretRef`). Replace it with `keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef = effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef(cp)` (helper defined in boundary 4), mirroring the DB override at lines 123-124: managed mode points the child at the operator-owned Secret `{Name: adminPasswordSecretName(cp), Key: "password"}`; brownfield passes the user-supplied ref through untouched. `cp.Spec` is never mutated.

**4. This issue must repoint every cp-side reader of the admin password — a consumer the source issue's sketch does not call out, but its "converges to Ready" criterion requires (review pattern: "Trace every spec field to all its consumers").**
Two production code paths read the admin password by the cp-level ref today:
- `readAdminPassword` (`reconcile_korc.go:885-893`) resolves `cp.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential.PasswordSecretRef` and is called by `reconcileKORC` (line 356, to render the password-based clouds.yaml and the rotation hash) and by `computeAdminPasswordHash` (line 873, shared with the CredentialRotation reconciler).
- `controlPlaneSecretNameExtractor` (`controlplane_controller.go:201`) indexes ControlPlanes by `PasswordSecretRef.Name` so the Secret watch (`SetupWithManager`, lines 287-289) wakes the CP on admin-password rotation.

On a production cluster with the static manifest deleted, a Secret named `keystone-admin` never exists; `reconcileKORC` would loop forever on `KORCReady=False / WaitingForAdminPassword` (`reconcile_korc.go:357-368`) and the ControlPlane would never reach `Ready`. The kind shim masks this on the dev path only. Therefore add one helper in `reconcile_adminpassword.go`:

```go
// effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef returns the SecretRef every cp-side consumer
// reads the admin password from: the operator-owned per-CP Secret in managed
// mode (Database.ClusterRef != nil), the user-supplied spec ref in brownfield.
func effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef(cp *c5c3v1alpha1.ControlPlane) commonv1.SecretRefSpec
```

and use it in exactly three places: the `reconcile_keystone.go:131` override (boundary 3), `readAdminPassword` (replace the `ref :=` line at `reconcile_korc.go:886`; the `key == "" → "password"` fallback stays), and `controlPlaneSecretNameExtractor` (replace the `name :=` line at `controlplane_controller.go:201`, and update its doc comment plus the `ControlPlaneSecretNameIndexKey` doc comment at lines 32-39 to say the indexed value is the *effective* name — the index-key string constant itself stays unchanged, it is an opaque indexer label). `mark_eso_managed` already stamps the per-CP admin path in the seeder (CC-0109), so the rotation PushSecret keeps overwriting the same key the new ExternalSecret reads — the rotation loop stays closed with zero keystone-operator changes.

Ordering remains benign exactly as the issue states: `reconcileKORC` runs after Keystone (`controlplane_controller.go:123-127`) and already tolerates a not-yet-synced password via `WaitingForAdminPassword`; since `reconcileAdminPassword` gates on ExternalSecret readiness before Keystone even projects, the Secret exists by the time KORC reads it.

**5. Production removal and the kind-only shims (Secrets 1 and 2).**
- Delete `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-admin.yaml`, `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/mariadb-root-password.yaml`, and `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml`; reduce `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml` to `resources: [clustersecretstore.yaml]` and update its header comment (the `externalsecrets/` directory disappears).
- Add `deploy/kind/infrastructure/keystone-admin-externalsecret.yaml`: ExternalSecret `keystone-admin` in `openstack`, store `openbao-cluster-store`, target `keystone-admin` / `CreationPolicy: Owner`, one data entry `password ← bootstrap/openstack/controlplane-keystone/admin` (property `password`) — byte-level shape of today's production manifest, with a header comment mirroring `deploy/kind/infrastructure/keystone-db-externalsecret.yaml`: standalone Keystone CRs (Quick Start, the `tests/e2e/keystone/*`, `tests/e2e-chaos/*`, and `tests/tempest/*` fixtures, plus `hack/run-tempest.sh`/`hack/ci-run-tempest.sh` which extract the admin password from this Secret) reference `keystone-admin` by name; ControlPlane deployments use the operator-projected per-CP ExternalSecret instead.
- Add `deploy/kind/infrastructure/mariadb-root-password-externalsecret.yaml`: ExternalSecret `mariadb-root-password` in `openstack`, one data entry `password ← infrastructure/mariadb` (property `root-password`), same content as today's production manifest, with a header explaining the Secret 2 rationale (next boundary).
- List both new files in `deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml` next to the existing `keystone-db-externalsecret.yaml` entry, extending that entry's explanatory comment block (review pattern: "Document intentional environment divergence in overlays").

**6. Secret 2 (`mariadb-root-password`) is relocation-only — option 1 of the source issue, decided here.**
This is not a per-ControlPlane service credential, so it gets no `reconcileDBCredentials`-style projection. The verified consumer set: the standalone Flux MariaDB baseline's `rootPasswordSecretKeyRef` (`deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml:43-45`); the `hack/deploy-infra.sh` force-sync loop (line 1216) and Step 8 wait (lines 1223-1224); and the `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` assertion (the ExternalSecret Ready block around lines 190-198). The operator-managed path is already dead weight: `ensureMariaDB` (`reconcile_infrastructure.go:166-173`) sets no `rootPasswordSecretKeyRef` at all — mariadb-operator generates its own root Secret per managed cluster. Relocating the manifest to the kind overlay keeps every consumer green on kind. One consequence must be documented, not coded around: a non-kind deployment of the Flux MariaDB baseline now has to provide the `mariadb-root-password` Secret itself (own ExternalSecret or plain Secret) — add this prerequisite note to `docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md`, which already discusses the root-password race at lines 832-835 and the source Secret at line 593. The fuller option (operator-owned root credential with per-cluster path and PushSecret mirroring) is a Non-Goal, adjacent to #439.

**7. Secret 3 (`k-orc-clouds-yaml`, orc-system copy) is consumer elimination — option 1 of the source issue, decided here.**
Verified consumer inventory: the K-ORC deployment itself has **no** global default clouds.yaml mount — `deploy/flux-system/releases/k-orc.yaml` (comment lines 20-26) documents that the upstream installer applied from `./dist` has no `globalCloudConfig` knob; the comment's "remains for any global default K-ORC mounts" describes a mount that no longer exists. The per-resource credential path is untouched: K-ORC resolves each CR's `CloudCredentialsRef` against the operator-owned per-CR ExternalSecret `k-orc-clouds-yaml` in the ControlPlane's own namespace (`ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret`, `reconcile_korc.go:1190-1230`, CC-0114). The only live consumer of the orc-system copy is the e2e verify Job `tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone/01-openstack-verify-job.yaml` (namespace `orc-system`, mounts Secret `k-orc-clouds-yaml`, lines 26 and 50-53). Changes:
- Repoint the verify Job to `namespace: openstack` (the ControlPlane namespace) mounting the per-CR Secret `k-orc-clouds-yaml` that lives there; rewrite its header comment accordingly.
- In `tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone/chainsaw-test.yaml`: drop the orc-system-copy assertion noted at lines 248-250 ("then confirm the orc-system copy K-ORC's globalCloudConfig mounts also materialised"), change `ORC_NS` usage in the verify-job step (lines 303-312), the catch-block diagnostics (`kubectl describe externalsecret k-orc-clouds-yaml -n orc-system`, line 329; the Job log dumps at line 335), and the `finally` cleanup (lines 342-344) to the ControlPlane namespace. The K-ORC controller-log diagnostic (line 333) stays on `orc-system` — the controller still runs there.
- Delete the manifest and its kustomization entry (boundary 5); update the stale cross-references: `deploy/flux-system/releases/k-orc.yaml` comment lines 20-26, and the `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml` comment that justifies co-managing `orc-system` "for the ESO ExternalSecret" (the namespace declaration itself stays — the installer's resources still land there).
- No kind shim for this one: nothing on the kind path consumes the orc-system copy (`hack/deploy-infra.sh` waits only on `openstack`-namespace ExternalSecrets; `infra-stack-health` never asserts it).

**8. `hack/deploy-infra.sh`: wait list unchanged, stale comment replaced.**
The Step 8 force-sync loop (`for es in keystone-admin keystone-db mariadb-root-password`, line 1216) and the `wait_for_externalsecrets "openstack" … keystone-admin keystone-db mariadb-root-password` call (lines 1223-1224) keep working verbatim — the kind shims materialize the same three ExternalSecrets in `openstack`. The only edit is the stale parenthetical at lines 1187-1191 ("The static keystone-admin ExternalSecret is pinned to the default identity; a CONTROLPLANE_NAME override also requires editing that manifest until issue #412 replaces it with per-CR templating…"): replace it with a note that the admin-password ExternalSecret is now operator-projected per-CP (`reconcileAdminPassword`) and the kind overlay shim pins only the default identity for standalone flows, so `CONTROLPLANE_NAME` overrides no longer require manifest edits on the ControlPlane path.

**9. Seeder and policies: comment-only.**
`deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh` already seeds the per-CP admin path inside the `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` loop (`admin_path="kv-v2/bootstrap/${cp_ns}/${keystone_name}/admin"`) and marks it ESO-managed; the flat legacy seeds are already gone (CC-0112/CC-0116). Update only the inline comment that calls the path "the bootstrap source the keystone-admin ExternalSecret reads" to name the operator-projected ExternalSecret plus the kind shim. `deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl` (`kv-v2/data/bootstrap/*` read grant, with the existing CC-0112/CC-0116 verification-comment convention) and `eso-control-plane.hcl` (`bootstrap/*`, `openstack/*`, `infrastructure/*` read grants) already cover every path shape this issue uses — append a verification comment in the established style stating the operator-projected admin-password ExternalSecret reads `bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}/admin` under the existing grant, read-only, no widening (review pattern: "Cross-reference access policies against all consumers"). The `setup-auth.sh` role bindings are untouched.

**10. Unit, integration, and Helm test changes — spelled out because they are the bulk of the diff.**
- New `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_adminpassword_test.go` mirroring `reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go` exactly: fixtures `adminPasswordManagedControlPlane()` / `adminPasswordBrownfieldControlPlane()` (or reuse of the existing `dbCredManagedControlPlane` shape), helpers analog to `getDBCredES`/`readyDBCredES`, and five tests — `TestReconcileAdminPassword_Managed_CreatesExternalSecret` (name, namespace, store ref, `CreatePolicyOwner`, single `password` data entry with `RemoteRef.Key == adminPasswordRemoteKeyFor(cp)` and `Property == "password"`, controller owner-ref), `TestReconcileAdminPassword_NotReady_SetsConditionFalseAndRequeues` (condition `False`/`WaitingForAdminPasswordSecret`, `RequeueAfter == adminPasswordRequeueAfter`), `TestReconcileAdminPassword_Ready_SetsConditionTrue`, `TestReconcileAdminPassword_Brownfield_NoExternalSecret_ReadyTrue` (no ES created, condition `True` immediately, `cp.Spec` untouched), and `TestAdminPasswordRemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane` — a table test over {same name/different namespaces, different names/same namespace} asserting no pair of secret names or remote keys collides and none equals the shared literal `keystone-admin`.
- `reconcile_keystone_test.go`: add `TestReconcileKeystone_ManagedOverridesAdminPasswordSecretRef` and `TestReconcileKeystone_BrownfieldLeavesSuppliedAdminPasswordRef`, mirroring the DB pair at lines 397-449 (override asserts child name == `adminPasswordSecretName(cp)`, key `password`, and `cp.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential.PasswordSecretRef` unchanged). Fix `TestReconcileKeystone_ClusterRefsDerivedFromInfrastructure` line 170, which currently asserts the managed child carries `keystone-admin` — it must now expect `adminPasswordSecretName(cp)`.
- `controlplane_controller_test.go` / `setupwithmanager_test.go`: update extractor/indexer expectations to the effective name (managed fixtures index `adminPasswordSecretName(cp)`, brownfield fixtures the spec ref).
- `reconcile_korc_test.go` and `reconcile_credentialrotation_test.go`: any managed-mode fixture that pre-creates the admin password Secret under the spec-ref name must rename it to `adminPasswordSecretName(cp)` (or switch the fixture to brownfield) so `readAdminPassword` keeps resolving.
- `integration_test.go`: the spec-level ref fixture (line 153) and the two pre-created `keystone-admin` Secrets (lines 317, 619) move to `adminPasswordSecretName(cp)`; insert a phase between Infrastructure and Keystone that waits for the operator-created admin ExternalSecret, asserts `RemoteRef.Key == adminPasswordRemoteKeyFor(cp)` and the controller owner-ref, then calls `simulators.SimulateExternalSecretSync` — the exact pattern of the DB-credential phase at lines 332-345 (`SimulateExternalSecretSync` patches only ES status, so the pre-created plain Secret remains the cleartext source for `readAdminPassword`). Extend the projected-spec assertions at lines 448 and 588 with the admin-ref analog.
- Helm: no chart change. The ClusterRole already grants full CRUD on `externalsecrets` (`operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl:195-199`) and `tests/clusterrole_test.yaml:181-212` already asserts create/update for `reconcileDBCredentials`; the new sub-reconciler creates the same resource kind, so nothing is extended.

**11. New Chainsaw e2e suite plus updates to the existing ones.**
- New `tests/e2e/c5c3/admin-password-scoping/{chainsaw-test.yaml,00-controlplane-cr.yaml}` mirroring `tests/e2e/c5c3/db-credential-scoping/` structurally: the same belt-and-braces CRD/ClusterSecretStore presence guard with SKIP-exit-0, single consolidated bash script step, applying the canonical ControlPlane and asserting the ExternalSecret `controlplane-keystone-admin-credentials` in `openstack` (owner-ref `ControlPlane/controlplane`, `remoteRef.key == bootstrap/openstack/controlplane-keystone/admin`, property `password`) and the materialized Secret of the same name carrying a non-empty `password` key (review pattern: "Assert full spec, not just resource existence").
- `tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone/`: the verify-Job and orc-system changes from boundary 7.
- `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml`: assertions for `keystone-admin` and `mariadb-root-password` (around lines 168-198) stay valid on kind; extend the CC-0116 explanatory comment block at lines 145-155 to state that these two are now also kind-only shims.

**12. Documentation (per repo convention, docs carry no CC-NNNN/REQ-NNN IDs — verified: `docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-reconciler.md` contains none today).**
- `docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-reconciler.md`: sub-reconciler chain diagram (~line 236) gains the AdminPassword box between DBCredentials and Keystone; the sub-condition list (line 335) gains `AdminPasswordReady`; a new `### reconcileAdminPassword` section mirrors the `### reconcileDBCredentials` section (lines 392-428: condition, gate, projects/owns row naming `{controlplane.Name}-keystone-admin-credentials` and the RemoteKey `bootstrap/{ns}/{name}-keystone/admin`, brownfield no-op, condition table); the owned-resources table (line 789) gains the admin ExternalSecret row; the sub_reconciler→condition table (line 864) gains `AdminPassword | AdminPasswordReady`; the secret-flow diagram (lines 684, 699) and the file-layout listing (lines 951-961) gain `reconcile_adminpassword.go`; the test-files table (~line 920) gains the `reconcile_adminpassword_test.go` row; the integration-test prose (lines 895-905) gains the new simulated dependency.
- `docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md`: the `passwordSecretRef` row (line 364) and the managed-mode callouts (lines 244-247, 747, 762) document that in managed mode the operator projects the per-CP admin ExternalSecret and overrides the projected child's `bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef`, while the cp-level default `keystone-admin` remains the brownfield/spec-level default.
- `docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md`: the architecture diagram (line 35) and directory tree (lines 104-105, which list the deleted manifests); the Bootstrap Secrets consumer column (lines 415-417 — admin path consumer becomes the operator-created ExternalSecret plus the kind shim); the ESO Integration table (lines 432-437) — replace the static `keystone-admin` row (whose "K8s Secret Name" cell currently reads `keystone-admin-credentials` and is wrong against today's manifest target `keystone-admin`; the rewrite fixes it) with the operator-owned row plus kind-shim rows for `keystone-admin` and `mariadb-root-password` in the style of the existing `keystone-db (kind only)` row, and rewrite the trailing notes (lines 439-456).
- `docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md`: orc-system copy sections (lines 72, 452, 676-698) rewritten for the removal; the MariaDB root-password prerequisite note (lines 593, 832-835) per boundary 6.
- `docs/quick-start-controlplane.md`: lines 58, 136, 181 — the comment block and the `OS_PASSWORD` extraction (`kubectl get secret keystone-admin …`) switch to the operator-owned Secret `controlplane-keystone-admin-credentials` so the command holds for any identity.
- Touch-up sweep: `grep -rn "keystone-admin\|mariadb-root-password\|k-orc-clouds-yaml" docs/` and update any remaining mention of the *static production manifests* (verified hits include `docs/quick-start-extended.md` and `docs/reference/infrastructure/e2e-deployment.md`); mentions of the standalone Secret names in keystone-operator docs/guides stay — the kind shims keep those names real.

**Motivation:**

The three manifests under `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/` are the last static, single-identity credential shims in the production deploy stack. Each hardcodes the default Quick Start identity — `bootstrap/openstack/controlplane-keystone/admin`, the `openstack` namespace, the `controlplane` name — so a ControlPlane under any other name or namespace converges only if someone edits manifests in lockstep with `CONTROLPLANE_NAME`. `hack/deploy-infra.sh:1187-1191` documents exactly this coupling as a known wart.

The `keystone-admin` removal is the load-bearing one, and it deadlocks if done naively: the keystone-operator's `SecretsReady` gate requires a Ready **ExternalSecret** behind the admin Secret (`reconcile_secrets.go:119-133`), and `reconcileKORC` blocks on reading the password (`reconcile_korc.go:356-368`) — deleting the manifest without operator projection plus the effective-ref repoint leaves every production ControlPlane stuck at `SecretsReady=False / WaitingForAdminCredentials` and `KORCReady=False / WaitingForAdminPassword`. CC-0112 already moved the write side per-CR (seeder and rotation PushSecret both target `bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}/admin`); this issue moves the read side, completing the loop the same way CC-0116 (PR #442) completed it for the DB credential.

Finishing all three shrinks production `deploy/eso/` to the `ClusterSecretStore` — genuinely cluster-scoped infrastructure — and unblocks multi-tenant ControlPlanes on the default deploy path. The standalone/Quick-Start flows keep working through kind-only shims, the move CC-0116 already demonstrated for `keystone-db`: the static identity stays in the dev overlay, out of the production stack.

**Affected Areas:**

- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_adminpassword.go` (new: suffix const, `adminPasswordSecretName`, `adminPasswordRemoteKeyFor`, `adminPasswordExternalSecret`, `effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef`, `reconcileAdminPassword`)
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_adminpassword_test.go` (new: five tests mirroring `reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go`)
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/controlplane_controller.go` (condition constant at 46-54, `subConditionTypes` at 58-65, chain insertion between lines 115 and 117, `controlPlaneSecretNameExtractor` at 201 + doc comments at 32-39/188-193)
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/instrumentation.go` (map entry at 40-47)
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/requeue_intervals.go` (`adminPasswordRequeueAfter`)
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_keystone.go` (line 131 override via `effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef`)
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc.go` (`readAdminPassword` at 885-893 resolves the effective ref)

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] `deploy/eso/` contains only `clustersecretstore.yaml` and `kustomization.yaml`, and the kustomization lists only the ClusterSecretStore; `kubectl kustomize deploy/eso` renders no ExternalSecret. The standalone flows are served by kind-only shims `deploy/kind/infrastructure/keystone-admin-externalsecret.yaml` and `mariadb-root-password-externalsecret.yaml` (mirroring `keystone-db-externalsecret.yaml`'s header style), listed in that overlay's kustomization.
- [ ] A managed-mode ControlPlane with an arbitrary name/namespace converges to `Ready=True` without editing any static manifest: `reconcileAdminPassword` projects an owner-referenced ExternalSecret named `{name}-keystone-admin-credentials` in `cp.Namespace` reading `bootstrap/{namespace}/{name}-keystone/admin` (property `password`, store `openbao-cluster-store`, `CreationPolicy: Owner`); the projected Keystone child's `bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef` and the KORC-side `readAdminPassword` both resolve `effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef(cp)`, so the keystone-operator `SecretsReady` gate and `reconcileKORC` both pass against the operator-owned Secret.
- [ ] `TestAdminPasswordRemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane` passes: for two ControlPlanes with the same name in different namespaces and different names in one namespace, no pair of admin Secret names or `bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}/admin` remote keys collides, and none equals the shared literal `keystone-admin`.
- [ ] A brownfield ControlPlane (`Database.ClusterRef == nil`) projects no admin-password ExternalSecret, leaves the user-supplied `PasswordSecretRef` untouched end-to-end (cp spec, projected child, `readAdminPassword`), and reports `AdminPasswordReady=True` immediately — asserted by `TestReconcileAdminPassword_Brownfield_NoExternalSecret_ReadyTrue` and `TestReconcileKeystone_BrownfieldLeavesSuppliedAdminPasswordRef`.
- [ ] `AdminPasswordReady` is a member of both `subConditionTypes` and `subReconcilerConditionTypes`; `TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames` and the `TestInstrumentSubReconciler_*` suite pass; the aggregate `Ready` is `True` only when `AdminPasswordReady` is `True`; the sub-reconciler runs after `DBCredentials` and before `reconcileKeystone` in `Reconcile`.
- [ ] `make deploy-infra` on kind still passes Step 8 unchanged: the force-sync/wait lists in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` (lines 1216, 1223-1224) still reference `keystone-admin keystone-db mariadb-root-password`, now materialized by the kind shims; the stale comment block at lines 1187-1191 is replaced; `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health` still asserts all three ExternalSecrets Ready.
- [ ] OpenBao policies remain read-only and unwidened: `git diff` over `deploy/openbao/policies/*.hcl` shows comment-only changes; the new verification comments follow the existing CC-0116-style convention in `eso-management.hcl`.
- [ ] Unit/integration suites pass with the additions from Description boundary 10, including the corrected assertion in `TestReconcileKeystone_ClusterRefsDerivedFromInfrastructure` and the new integration phase that waits for and sync-simulates the admin ExternalSecret; the Helm test suite passes untouched (existing `clusterrole_test.yaml` externalsecrets coverage suffices).
- [ ] `tests/e2e/c5c3/admin-password-scoping/` exists with the presence-guarded single-script structure of `db-credential-scoping` and, on a stack-equipped cluster, asserts the operator-owned ExternalSecret shape and the materialized Secret's `password` key; `tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone` passes with the verify Job running in the ControlPlane namespace against the per-CR `k-orc-clouds-yaml` Secret and no remaining orc-system ExternalSecret assertion.
- [ ] Docs updated per Description boundary 12, with no CC-NNNN/REQ-NNN IDs introduced into `docs/`; `grep -rn "deploy/eso/externalsecrets" docs/` returns no stale references to the deleted manifests.

**Non-Goals:**

- **Operator-owned MariaDB root credential** (Secret 2 option 2: per-cluster `infrastructure/mariadb/{ns}/{name}` path, PushSecret mirroring, `rootPasswordSecretKeyRef` wiring in `ensureMariaDB`) — only worth it if root-credential backup/rotation is in scope; adjacent to #439 (dynamic per-tenant MariaDB secret engine) and deferred there.
- **An operator-created, non-owned ExternalSecret in `orc-system`** (Secret 3 option 2) — reintroduces the "which ControlPlane owns the global default" ambiguity that #412/CC-0112/CC-0116 removed; the consumer is eliminated instead.
- **Changing the webhook defaults** — `DefaultAdminPasswordSecretName = "keystone-admin"` stays the spec-level/brownfield default, exactly as `DefaultDatabaseSecretName = "keystone-db"` survived CC-0116.
- **Any keystone-operator change** — the `SecretsReady` gate and the Model B rotation PushSecret already operate on whatever ref the child carries and on the per-CR OpenBao path; the override makes them point at the operator-owned objects with zero code change there.
- **Porting the keystone operator's `TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllCallSites` AST drift guard to c5c3** — c5c3's `instrumentation.go` comments already cite it aspirationally; adding the guard is independent hygiene work, not part of this change.
- **Relocating or restructuring the Flux MariaDB baseline itself** (`deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml`) — only its root-password sourcing prerequisite is documented.
- **A `CONTROLPLANE_NAMESPACE` knob for `deploy-infra.sh`** — the canonical kind ControlPlane stays `openstack/controlplane` (`deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml`); multi-identity seeding already exists via `KORC_CONTROLPLANES`.

requirements.description: The AdminPassword sub-reconciler SHALL, in managed mode (cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef != nil), create-or-update an owner-referenced ExternalSecret named adminPasswordSecretName(cp) in childNamespace(cp), backed by ClusterSecretStore 'openbao-cluster-store', with target.creationPolicy Owner, refreshInterval 1h, and a single data entry {SecretKey: 'password', RemoteRef: {Key: adminPasswordRemoteKeyFor(cp), Property: 'password'}}.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the merged CC-0116 DB-credential projection so the admin password is delivered per-ControlPlane from OpenBao instead of via a single static manifest.

scenarios.when: reconcileAdminPassword runs for a managed-mode ControlPlane
scenarios.then: an ExternalSecret named '{name}-keystone-admin-credentials' exists in cp.Namespace with the documented store ref, Owner policy, and single password data entry
and_then: the ExternalSecret carries a controller owner reference of Kind 'ControlPlane' and Name cp.Name
RemoteRef.Key == adminPasswordRemoteKeyFor(cp) and RemoteRef.Property == 'password'

scenarios.name: Builder is pure (no owner ref set in builder)
scenarios.when: adminPasswordExternalSecret(cp) is called directly
scenarios.then: it returns an *esov1.ExternalSecret with no owner reference (the reconciler sets it in the CreateOrUpdate mutate closure)
and_then: the returned object is usable for shape assertions in unit tests

scenarios.then: AdminPasswordReady is set False with reason 'ExternalSecretError' and the error is returned
and_then: no requeue interval is set on the error path (the controller-runtime error backoff applies)

requirements.description: adminPasswordRemoteKeyFor(cp) SHALL return fmt.Sprintf("bootstrap/%s/%s/admin", cp.Namespace, keystoneName(cp)) and adminPasswordSecretName(cp) SHALL return keystoneName(cp) + "-admin-credentials"; the remote key is keystone-name-scoped (NOT cp-name-scoped like the DB path) so it matches the seeder and the keystone-operator Model B rotation PushSecret.
requirements.rationale: The read path must land on exactly the key the seeder writes ('kv-v2/bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}-keystone/admin' via keystone_name) and the rotation PushSecret overwrites, so the rotation loop stays closed with zero keystone-operator changes.

and_then: this matches today's deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-admin.yaml remoteRef.key and property 'password'

requirements.description: The AdminPassword sub-reconciler SHALL drive the AdminPasswordReady condition: while the projected ExternalSecret is not Ready it SHALL set AdminPasswordReady=False reason 'WaitingForAdminPasswordSecret' and requeue after adminPasswordRequeueAfter (10s); once Ready it SHALL set AdminPasswordReady=True reason 'AdminPasswordReady'; a WaitForExternalSecret error SHALL set False reason 'ExternalSecretError'.
requirements.rationale: Gating on the ExternalSecret's Ready condition (not the plain Secret) is what makes the keystone-operator SecretsReady gate satisfiable; mirrors reconcileDBCredentials exactly.

requirements.rationale: Brownfield users own the admin Secret out-of-band; the operator must not reference OpenBao or the ClusterSecretStore, exactly as reconcileDBCredentials handles its brownfield early-exit (same discriminator).

review_criteria: BLOCKER (correctness): A managed ControlPlane with an arbitrary name/namespace converges to Ready=True with no static-manifest edit; reconcileAdminPassword projects an owner-referenced ExternalSecret {name}-keystone-admin-credentials in cp.Namespace reading bootstrap/{ns}/{name}-keystone/admin (property password, store openbao-cluster-store, CreationPolicy Owner).

BLOCKER (correctness): effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef is used at all THREE readers (reconcile_keystone.go:131, reconcile_korc.go:886, controlplane_controller.go:201) so the keystone-operator SecretsReady gate and reconcileKORC both pass against the operator-owned Secret on a cluster with the static manifest deleted.

BLOCKER (correctness): Brownfield (Database.ClusterRef == nil) projects no ExternalSecret, leaves PasswordSecretRef untouched end-to-end (cp spec, projected child, readAdminPassword), and reports AdminPasswordReady=True immediately.

BLOCKER (status contract): conditionTypeAdminPasswordReady is a member of both subConditionTypes and subReconcilerConditionTypes; the aggregate Ready is True only when AdminPasswordReady is True; the sub-reconciler runs after DBCredentials and before reconcileKeystone; no collision with conditionTypeAdminCredentialReady.

BLOCKER (tests): All Go unit/integration suites pass with the additions, including the corrected TestReconcileKeystone_ClusterRefsDerivedFromInfrastructure assertion and the new integration phase that waits-for and sync-simulates the admin ExternalSecret; the new five-test reconcile_adminpassword_test.go mirrors reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go.

CRITICAL (manifests): kubectl kustomize deploy/eso renders only the ClusterSecretStore (directory deploy/eso/externalsecrets/ removed); the kind shims keystone-admin-externalsecret.yaml and mariadb-root-password-externalsecret.yaml are listed in the kind kustomization and materialize the Secrets standalone flows reference; `make deploy-infra` Step 8 wait/force-sync lists are unchanged.

CRITICAL (security/policy): git diff over deploy/openbao/policies/*.hcl is comment-only and unwidened; verification comments follow the CC-0112/CC-0116 convention; setup-auth.sh role bindings untouched.

CRITICAL (e2e): tests/e2e/c5c3/admin-password-scoping asserts the operator-owned ExternalSecret shape and materialized Secret password key behind a presence guard; full-controlplane-keystone passes with the verify Job in the ControlPlane namespace against the per-CR k-orc-clouds-yaml Secret and no remaining orc-system ExternalSecret assertion.

CRITICAL (Helm/RBAC): No chart change required — the existing ClusterRole externalsecrets CRUD grant (_helpers.tpl:195-199) and clusterrole_test.yaml:181-212 already cover the new sub-reconciler's resource kind; confirm no Helm test regression.

MINOR (docs): Docs updated per Description boundary 12 with no CC-NNNN/REQ-NNN IDs introduced into docs/; grep -rn 'deploy/eso/externalsecrets' docs/ returns no stale references; the openbao-bootstrap.md ESO Integration table's wrong keystone-admin-credentials cell is corrected.

MINOR (surgical): The defaulting webhook is unchanged; no operator-keystone code change; the ControlPlaneSecretNameIndexKey string constant is unchanged (only its doc comments); every changed line traces to this feature.

MINOR (lint/types): go vet and golangci-lint clean (note the //nolint:gosec G101 annotation on adminPasswordSecretNameSuffix); gofumpt formatting; no inline condition-type string literals (use the constant).
implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURE: This adds the seventh ControlPlane sub-reconciler, a 1:1 structural mirror of the merged CC-0116 DB-credentials pattern. It is backend-only (Go operator + IaC manifests + docs/tests) — there is NO frontend/hooks layer, so the fullstack-checklist's UI items do not apply.

SUB-RECONCILER CHAIN (after this change):
  Reconcile
    -> Infrastructure  (InfrastructureReady)
    -> DBCredentials   (DBCredentialsReady)        reconcile_dbcredentials.go
    -> AdminPassword   (AdminPasswordReady)   NEW  reconcile_adminpassword.go   <-- inserted here
    -> Keystone        (KeystoneReady)
    -> KORC            (KORCReady)
    -> AdminCredential (AdminCredentialReady)       (K-ORC ApplicationCredential — DIFFERENT credential)
    -> Catalog         (CatalogReady)
  setReadyCondition gates Ready on conditions.AllTrue(subConditionTypes...).

WHY ADMINPASSWORD RUNS BEFORE KEYSTONE: the keystone-operator's reconcileSecrets gates SecretsReady on the admin Secret being backed by a Ready ExternalSecret (WaitForExternalSecret(adminSecretKey), reason WaitingForAdminCredentials). reconcileAdminPassword must therefore create+ready the ExternalSecret before the Keystone child is projected to reference it.

DATA FLOW (managed mode, default identity):
  write-bootstrap-secrets.sh seeds kv-v2/bootstrap/openstack/controlplane-keystone/admin (property password), marks it ESO-managed (CC-0109)
      |
      v
  reconcileAdminPassword creates ExternalSecret 'controlplane-keystone-admin-credentials' (ns openstack) reading bootstrap/openstack/controlplane-keystone/admin via ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store
      |  ESO syncs -> Secret 'controlplane-keystone-admin-credentials' {password}
      v
  effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef(cp) -> {Name: 'controlplane-keystone-admin-credentials', Key: 'password'} consumed by:
      - reconcile_keystone.go:131  (projected child's bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef; keystone-operator SecretsReady gate satisfied)
      - reconcile_korc.go:886       (readAdminPassword cleartext -> clouds.yaml + rotation hash)
      - controlplane_controller.go:201 (indexer wakes the CP on rotation of this Secret)
      |
      v
  keystone-operator Model B rotation PushSecret (RemoteKey bootstrap/{keystone.Namespace}/{keystone.Name}/admin) overwrites the SAME key the ExternalSecret reads -> rotation loop closed, zero keystone-operator changes.

KEY DECISION — path scoping asymmetry: the admin remote key is keystone-name-scoped (bootstrap/{ns}/{keystoneName(cp)}/admin) whereas the DB key is cp-name-scoped (openstack/keystone/{ns}/{cp.Name}/db). This is NOT a copy-paste slip — it matches the seeder and the keystone-operator rotation PushSecret, and because the projected Keystone child is named keystoneName(cp) in cp.Namespace, the read path and the rotation write path land on the same key. Document this in the helper godoc.

KEY DECISION — brownfield discriminator: brownfield is keyed on Database.ClusterRef == nil, identical to reconcileDBCredentials, per the issue. (A managed database is the proxy for 'the operator owns the credentials'.) Leave a DECISION comment at the discriminator so reviewers can confirm the coupling is intentional.

KEY DECISION — per-CP-unique name is mandatory: childNamespace(cp) == cp.Namespace, so two ControlPlanes in one namespace would collide on a single 'keystone-admin' Secret — the exact multi-instance failure CC-0116 fixed. The '-admin-credentials' suffix on keystoneName(cp) makes the name collision-free.

PITFALLS:
  - SimulateExternalSecretSync patches ONLY the ExternalSecret .status (it does NOT create the backing Secret). The integration test must keep the pre-created plain Secret (renamed to adminPasswordSecretName(cp)) as the cleartext source for readAdminPassword.
  - The c5c3 'sub-condition gates integration Ready' constraint: adding AdminPasswordReady to subConditionTypes makes TestIntegration_FullReconcile time out until the new sub-reconciler is wired AND the integration test sync-simulates the admin ExternalSecret. Wire the integration phase in the same change.
  - TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllCallSites does NOT exist in c5c3 (only in the keystone operator); the c5c3 'CC-0089 drift-guard' maps to TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames + the TestInstrumentSubReconciler_* suite. Do not port the AST guard (Non-Goal).
  - Use the conditionTypeAdminPasswordReady CONSTANT everywhere (no inline 'AdminPasswordReady' literals) to satisfy the no-inline-literals discipline and avoid the AdminCredentialReady collision.
  - The //nolint:gosec G101 annotation belongs on adminPasswordSecretNameSuffix (Secret name suffix, not a credential), exactly like dbCredentialSecretNameSuffix.
  - Docs carry NO CC-NNNN/REQ-NNN IDs (verified: controlplane-reconciler.md contains none today).

OBSERVABILITY: the instrumentSubReconciler wrapper emits reconcile-duration histograms and error counters under sub_reconciler='AdminPassword' with condition_type='AdminPasswordReady' (resolved via subReconcilerConditionTypes), consistent with the other six sub-reconcilers. No new metrics are introduced.

SECURITY: no new OpenBao path or capability — the operator-projected admin ExternalSecret reads bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}/admin, already covered read-only by 'kv-v2/data/bootstrap/*' (eso-management.hcl) and 'bootstrap/*' (eso-control-plane.hcl). Policy changes are verification comments only.
